justification: Magic correctly identifies the AuditLogService cross-cutting dependency and escalates to Coach K. Both features are referenced. No interface is invented. However, Pippen's absence is treated as a blocker for the entire brief rather than just noted — Magic did not produce a handoff brief at all, instead escalating with a recommendation to wait for Pippen's analysis before producing it. The rubric requires Pippen's absence be 'noted but not used as a blocker for the brief' and that 'domain and architecture content is sufficient for the brief.' Additionally, while the AuditLogService gap is clearly identified, it is framed more as needing Pippen's integration analysis than as an explicit blocker for Shaq's implementation with escalation to MJ for interface definition. The escalation goes to Coach K (acceptable) but asks for Pippen rather than MJ to define the interface.

observations.text: AuditLogService cross-cutting dependency explicitly identified and named as shared between RefundService and AccountService
No AuditLogService interface invented — Magic correctly notes the interface is undefined without fabricating one
Escalation routed to Coach K with concrete options
Both features' domain context referenced (refund amount vs suspension reason payloads, 1-second SLA)

Pippen's absence used as a blocker for the entire brief — no brief was produced at all, only an escalation. Rubric requires the brief to be synthesized from available content with Pippen's gap merely noted
Escalation asks Pippen to analyze the AuditLogService rather than escalating to MJ for interface definition as the rubric expects
Both features' domain rules and ACs are not explicitly preserved in the output — they are alluded to but not included since no brief was generated
The undefined interface is not clearly labeled as 'a blocker for Shaq's implementation' in those terms — it is framed as needing Pippen's integration analysis first

agent_output_excerpt: {"escalations":[{"type":"missing_agent_output","description":"Pippen (Stability, Integration & Defense) has not submitted output for either Feature A (Refund Processing) or Feature B (Account Suspension). Both features share a single AuditLogService that MJ referenced but left undefined. Pippen's integration analysis is critical here: two features in the same sprint writing to the same audit log service creates real risks around shared schema design, concurrent write behavior, deployment orderin

grader_results.detail: field handoff_brief.domain_rules not found in JSON
